Class LoginAttemptService

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.service.LoginAttemptService
@Service public class LoginAttemptService extends Object
Tracks failed login attempts per username and enforces escalating lockout periods to protect against brute force attacks.

Lockout schedule per username (resets on successful login):

  • Attempts 1–5: no lockout
  • After 5 fails: 5-minute lockout
  • After 10 fails: 10-minute lockout
  • After 15 fails: 30-minute lockout
  • After 20 fails: permanent lockout — manual reset required

All state is held in memory. Lockout state is lost on application restart, which is intentional for a single-user desktop application.

A 5-second delay is applied on every failed attempt regardless of lockout state, slowing down automated credential stuffing attempts.

Time Complexity: O(1) for all operations (HashMap lookups). Space Complexity: O(u) where u = number of distinct usernames that have had at least one failed attempt.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getDummyHash()
    Returns the dummy BCrypt hash used for timing-safe authentication checks when no account exists for the submitted username.
    String
    getLockoutMessage(String username)
    Returns a human-readable message describing the current lockout state for the given username, suitable for display in the UI.
    int
    getRemainingAttempts(String username)
    Returns the number of remaining attempts before the next lockout tier is triggered for the given username.
    boolean
    isLockedOut(String username)
    Returns whether the given username is currently locked out.
    void
    recordFailedAttempt(String username)
    Records a failed login attempt for the given username and applies the 5-second delay.
    void
    resetAttempts(String username)
    Resets all failed attempt state for the given username.

  • Constructor Details

    • LoginAttemptService

      public LoginAttemptService()

  • Method Details

    • isLockedOut

      public boolean isLockedOut(String username)
      Returns whether the given username is currently locked out.

      For timed lockouts, the lock is automatically lifted if the lockout period has expired. For permanent lockouts (tier 4), this always returns true until resetAttempts(String) is called manually.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      username - the username or email to check
      Returns:
      true if the account is currently locked out

    • getLockoutMessage

      public String getLockoutMessage(String username)
      Returns a human-readable message describing the current lockout state for the given username, suitable for display in the UI.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      username - the username or email to check
      Returns:
      a descriptive lockout message, or an empty string if not locked out

    • recordFailedAttempt

      public void recordFailedAttempt(String username)
      Records a failed login attempt for the given username and applies the 5-second delay. If the attempt count reaches the tier threshold, a lockout is applied and the tier is incremented for the next breach.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      username - the username or email that failed authentication

    • resetAttempts

      public void resetAttempts(String username)
      Resets all failed attempt state for the given username. Called after a successful login or a manual admin reset.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      username - the username or email to reset

    • getDummyHash

      public String getDummyHash()
      Returns the dummy BCrypt hash used for timing-safe authentication checks when no account exists for the submitted username.

      Always running BCrypt ensures that the response time is identical whether or not an account exists, preventing username enumeration via timing side-channel analysis.

      Returns:
      the dummy BCrypt hash string

    • getRemainingAttempts

      public int getRemainingAttempts(String username)
      Returns the number of remaining attempts before the next lockout tier is triggered for the given username.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      username - the username or email to check
      Returns:
      remaining attempts before lockout, or MAX_ATTEMPTS_PER_TIER if no failed attempts have been recorded
